chunk_summary: This section discusses referral thresholds for pediatric obesity and metabolic dysfunction within the context of therapeutic diets and lifestyle interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for referral in pediatric obesity and metabolic dysfunction.
Identify the role of lifestyle interventions in managing pediatric obesity.
Learn about therapeutic diet strategies for metabolic health in children.
key_facts: Pediatric obesity is a significant risk factor for metabolic dysfunction, including type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease.
Referral thresholds often include BMI percentiles above the 95th percentile for age and sex.
Lifestyle interventions, including diet and physical activity, are first-line treatments for pediatric obesity.
Specialist referral may be indicated for children with comorbidities such as hypertension or dyslipidemia.
Therapeutic diets should be tailored to the child’s age, nutritional needs, and cultural preferences.
reference_quote: Referral thresholds for pediatric obesity often include BMI above the 95th percentile, particularly in the presence of comorbidities such as hypertension or dyslipidemia.
chunk_heading: THERAPEUTIC DIETS & SPECIAL INTERVENTIONS;LIFESTYLE INTERVENTION AND
heading_path: THERAPEUTIC DIETS & SPECIAL INTERVENTIONS;LIFESTYLE INTERVENTION AND
